programs.title: Shocking dishes
programs.desc: This time, Jarosław Nowak's goal is Kazakhstan.It is a country of contrasts, where you can see wild, green steppes and, on the other hand, modern, rapidly developing cities.First, the host visits Almaty, where he tries to discover what Kazakhs consider the strangest delicacy.He sees a traditional slaughterhouse and the world's largest camel farm.His plate includes sheep's head, camel meat and horse meat, which the local population considers to be Kazakh doping.According to culinary tradition and ancient customs, no part of the dish can be wasted, and the preparation of the dish itself is a real celebration.

programs.title: The Dead Files
programs.desc: Medium Amy Allan and retired homicide detective Steve DiSchiavi team up to investigate unexplained paranormal phenomena in haunted locations across the United States.

programs.title: Woman at the end of the world
programs.desc:
The program returns to the air after a break.Over the last few months, Martyna Wojciechowska and her team have traveled all over the world to look for inspiring women who will talk about what is currently most important.For some topics, the host had to wait many years, but for the good stuff, it's definitely worth it.The latest season will bring meetings with the oldest and youngest heroines in the history of the show.Viewers will see episodes shot in eight countries and on five continents.However, it is not about records or the number of kilometers traveled.The most important thing is meeting another person, time spent together, intimate conversation, and the truth about the world
This time Martyna goes to Florida, which is Americans' favorite place to spend their retirement.They move there to spend the autumn of their lives walking along the seaside and watching sunsets.However, for the heroines of this episode - Donna and her bandmates - this is definitely not enough.The Calendar Girls group consists of thirty women aged 52 to 82.The women were united by their passion for dance and physical activity.They meet for training several times a week and take part in almost 200 performances a year.With their colorful and joyful repertoire, they entertain residents of retirement homes and financially support the training of guide dogs.The heroines of the episode prove that old age is joy.

programs.title: Woman at the end of the world
programs.desc: The program returns to the air after a break.Over the last few months, Martyna Wojciechowska and her team have traveled all over the world to look for inspiring women who will talk about what is currently most important.For some topics, the host had to wait many years, but for the good stuff, it's definitely worth it.The latest season will bring meetings with the oldest and youngest heroines in the history of the show.Viewers will see episodes shot in eight countries and on five continents.However, it is not about records or the number of kilometers traveled.The most important thing is meeting another person, time spent together, intimate conversation, and the truth about the world.

programs.title: Woman at the end of the world
programs.desc: Reports showing the stories of women living in Africa, Asia and America.The heroines have different professions and follow different religions.Presenting their fate is only a pretext to show the exoticism, traditions and culture of the countries in which they live.Martyna Wojciechowska, who kept a diary from these extraordinary journeys, experienced their joys and sorrows together with her interlocutors and participated in their everyday activities.This allowed her to understand contrasts and learn about different approaches to femininity.
